mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is stable or falling while FEDFUNDS is near zero, real discount-rate pressure stays contained and equity multiples expand. This combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because valuation support dominates any weak-growth signal.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-rate disinflation instances do not outperform baseline on 20d returns, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of such cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates deteriorating household income expectations and lower risk appetite. That labor stress should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, especially when payroll growth is not offsetting the unemployment increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE breakout events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if adding PAYEMS does not improve hit rate versus UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upward turn in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift near-term earnings expectations and support S&P 500 gains over the next week. The effect is strongest when retail sales are accelerating rather than merely steady.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average and rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration events fail to produce positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in INDPRO with only modest inflation indicates real activity is improving without forcing tighter financial conditions. That mix should be bullish for equities over the next month because higher output supports earnings while the inflation channel remains contained.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO growth with subdued CPI does not beat baseline 20d returns, or if the sign turns negative across a 3-year rolling window more than once.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent average signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical spending. That deterioration should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions follow the housing slump.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing contraction events are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 60d return is not below unconditional performance.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in FEDFUNDS above a tightening threshold raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and compresses equity multiples. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once the policy rate is clearly restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS above 4.5% is not associated with below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not negative in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both improve, the economy is generating both output and spending momentum, which should reinforce earnings revisions and lift equities over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when both series are rising together rather than diverging.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDPC1 and RSAFS improvements do not generate positive 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession labor repair phase, falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS confirm that payroll gains are absorbing slack. That combination should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because labor-market normalization boosts consumption and reduces downside growth fears.
failure_condition: Reject if declining unemployment plus payroll gains do not outperform baseline on 20d returns, or if the combined signal loses sign stability across any 3-year rolling window.
